pkg/slicesx.purpose: Thin extension layer on top of samber/lo providing error-aware map, diff, and early-exit iteration helpers that lo omits; all functions are generic and pure with no side effects or domain imports.

patterns.name: MapWithErr accumulates all errors before returning
patterns.description: MapWithErr uses errors.Join to collect errors from every element — it does NOT short-circuit on first failure. The result slice omits erroring elements; count may be less than input.
patterns.example: results, err := slicesx.MapWithErr(items, func(i Item) (Out, error) { return convert(i) })

patterns.name: Nil-in nil-out contract
patterns.description: Map and MapWithErr both return nil (not an empty slice) when given a nil input. Callers that require a non-nil empty slice must initialize the input first.

patterns.name: NewDiff argument order: base then new
patterns.description: NewDiff(base, new) wraps lo.Difference — additions are items in base not in new; removals are items in new not in base. Swapping arguments silently inverts additions and removals.
patterns.example: d := slicesx.NewDiff(oldIDs, newIDs); if d.InAdditions(id) { /* id was removed from new set */ }

patterns.name: ForEachUntilWithErr breaks on true, errors on err — these are independent signals
patterns.description: Callback returning (true, nil) breaks the loop without error; (false, err) stops with error. Both stop iteration but are semantically distinct.
patterns.example: slicesx.ForEachUntilWithErr(items, func(v Item, i int) (bool, error) { return v.Done, nil })

patterns.name: Prefer lo for operations it already covers
patterns.description: Functions like Filter, Contains, Uniq, Keys are already in samber/lo and must not be re-implemented in slicesx. Add here only when lo explicitly lacks the pattern.
patterns.example: // Use lo.Filter, lo.Contains — not a new slicesx.Filter

key_file_guides.role: Diff[T,S] value type wrapping lo.Difference with O(1) index maps for InAdditions/InRemovals/Has and HasChanged convenience method.
key_file_guides.watch_for: NewDiff argument order: first arg is base (source of additions), second arg is new (source of removals). Swapping inverts semantics.

key_file_guides.role: Map (no-error transform) and MapWithErr (error-accumulating transform) generic slice helpers.
key_file_guides.watch_for: MapWithErr skips erroring elements and continues — result slice may have fewer elements than input; returned error is errors.Join of all failures.

key_file_guides.role: EmptyAsNil normalises zero-length slices to nil for struct equality assertions.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Only intended for test code — using in production code can hide unintended empty-slice semantics.

key_file_guides.role: ForEachUntilWithErr iterates with early-exit via (breaks bool, err error) return from callback.
key_file_guides.watch_for: breaks and err are independent — (true, nil) breaks cleanly; (false, err) stops with error. Never assume breaks==true implies no error.

key_file_guides.role: UniqueGroupBy groups a collection and asserts uniqueness — returns (nil, false) if any key maps to more than one element.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Returns (nil, false) on collision rather than an error — callers must check the bool.

anti_patterns: Duplicating lo functions (Filter, Contains, Uniq) here — use lo directly
Using EmptyAsNil in production code — it is intended for test assertion normalisation only
Adding stateful or side-effectful helpers — all functions must be pure transforms
Assuming MapWithErr returns a result slice of the same length as input when errors occur — erroring elements are skipped

decisions.decision: Wrap lo rather than replace it
decisions.rationale: lo covers ~90% of slice operations; slicesx fills only the gaps (error-returning maps, diff with O(1) index, early-exit iteration) without duplicating the rest.

code_examples.scenario: Convert a slice of domain entities to API types, collecting all conversion errors before returning

pkg/sortx.purpose: Minimal single-file package defining the sortx.Order string enum (ASC/DESC) and default/none sentinels used as a shared sort-direction type across all domain list and query inputs.

patterns.name: Use OrderDefault for unspecified sort direction
patterns.description: When a caller does not supply a sort direction, default to sortx.OrderDefault (= OrderAsc). Never use the empty string literal directly.

patterns.name: IsDefaultValue detects absence, not ascending direction
patterns.description: Order.IsDefaultValue() returns true only when Order == OrderNone (empty string), not when Order == OrderAsc. Use it solely to detect 'no order specified'.

key_file_guides.role: Defines Order type and four constants: OrderAsc ('ASC'), OrderDesc ('DESC'), OrderDefault (= OrderAsc), OrderNone (empty string).
key_file_guides.watch_for: IsDefaultValue() checks for OrderNone, not OrderAsc — the name is acknowledged as misleading in a TODO comment. Do not use it to check 'is ascending'.

anti_patterns: Adding sort-field definitions or multi-column sort structs here — this package is intentionally a single primitive
Comparing Order to string literals 'ASC'/'DESC' directly — use the exported constants
Using IsDefaultValue() as a semantic check for 'is ascending' — it only detects the unset (empty) state

decisions.decision: Single file, single type, no sub-packages
decisions.rationale: Sort direction is a leaf primitive shared by many list inputs; keeping it minimal prevents coupling and circular imports.

pkg/statelessx.purpose: Adapter utilities for the qmuntal/stateless state machine library — provides type-safe action and condition wrappers (WithParameters, AllOf, BoolFn, Not) that integrate with models.Validator so state machine transitions validate their inputs before executing.

patterns.name: WithParameters wraps typed handlers with automatic validation
patterns.description: WithParameters[T models.Validator] adapts a func(ctx, T) error into the stateless ...any signature. It asserts args[0] to T and calls T.Validate() before invoking the inner function — panics if args is empty or the type assertion fails.
patterns.example: machine.Configure(stateGathering).OnEntry(statelessx.WithParameters[AdvanceInput](func(ctx context.Context, in AdvanceInput) error { return svc.advance(ctx, in) }))

patterns.name: AllOf runs all actions regardless of individual failures
patterns.description: AllOf(fn1, fn2, ...) runs every ActionFn even if earlier ones fail, then returns errors.Join of all failures. Use when multiple side effects (save + publish) must all be attempted.

patterns.name: BoolFn and Not adapt plain bool functions to stateless guard signatures
patterns.description: BoolFn(fn func() bool) produces func(context.Context, ...any) bool accepted by stateless guards; Not inverts a plain bool func. BoolFn discards ctx and args — not suitable for guards needing per-trigger parameters.

key_file_guides.role: Defines ActionFn type alias, AllOf combinator, and WithParameters generic adapter for stateless entry/exit actions.
key_file_guides.watch_for: WithParameters expects args[0] to implement models.Validator; passing a non-Validator type or empty args panics at runtime with a type assertion failure. Callers must not call Validate() manually before passing — WithParameters already calls it.

key_file_guides.role: BoolFn and Not helpers for stateless guard functions that only need a bool, discarding ctx and trigger args.
key_file_guides.watch_for: BoolFn discards both ctx and args — do not use for guards that need per-trigger parameter access.

anti_patterns: Calling models.Validator.Validate() manually before passing to WithParameters — it already calls Validate() internally
Using stateless ...any signatures directly instead of WithParameters when the trigger carries a typed argument — loses compile-time safety
Using BoolFn for guards that need access to the trigger's arguments — BoolFn ignores all args

decisions.decision: WithParameters constrains T to models.Validator rather than any
decisions.rationale: Forces callers to define Validate() on input types, catching structural errors at the state machine boundary before transitions commit any side effects.

code_examples.scenario: Register a state machine transition with a validated typed parameter

pkg/strcase.purpose: Minimal zero-dependency string-case conversion package providing SnakeToCamel and CamelToSnake for converting JSON field names or database column identifiers to and from Go naming conventions.

patterns.name: Only underscore is treated as word separator for SnakeToCamel
patterns.description: SnakeToCamel only splits on '_'; hyphens and slashes pass through unchanged. CamelToSnake only splits on uppercase runes.

patterns.name: First character case is preserved, not forced to upper
patterns.description: SnakeToCamel does not uppercase the first character — result starts lowercase. This is camelCase, not PascalCase.

key_file_guides.role: Two pure functions: SnakeToCamel and CamelToSnake with round-trip guarantee for well-formed identifiers (letters, digits, underscores, no consecutive underscores).
key_file_guides.watch_for: SnakeToCamel preserves the first character's case — it produces camelCase, not PascalCase. CamelToSnake does not insert underscore before the first character even if it is uppercase.

anti_patterns: Using this package for PascalCase conversion — SnakeToCamel preserves the first character's case
Expecting kebab-case or dot-notation to be handled — only underscore delimiters are understood by SnakeToCamel

decisions.rationale: Avoids pulling in a heavy strcase library for two simple transformations used in generated or utility code.

pkg/timeutil.purpose: Shared time-interval primitives (ClosedPeriod, OpenPeriod, StartBoundedPeriod, Recurrence, Timeline) used throughout billing, subscription, entitlement, and metering to reason about billable windows, recurrence schedules, and event timelines with explicit boundary semantics.

patterns.name: Three Contains variants with documented boundary semantics — pick the right one
patterns.description: Every period type implements Period: Contains (inclusive start, exclusive end — the default for billing windows), ContainsInclusive (both inclusive), ContainsExclusive (both exclusive). Mixing them silently changes charge window edges.
patterns.example:
period.Contains(t)          // [from, to) — default billing window
period.ContainsInclusive(t) // [from, to] — use for point-in-time snapshots

patterns.name: Recurrence is anchor-based; always construct via NewRecurrence or NewRecurrenceFromISODuration
patterns.description: Anchor can be any past or future point; iteration walks forward/backward from it. Constructors call Validate() — direct struct construction skips validation and may pass zero anchor or non-positive interval.
patterns.example:
rec, err := timeutil.NewRecurrenceFromISODuration(datetime.DurationMonth, anchorTime)
period, err := rec.GetPeriodAt(now)

patterns.name: Boundary enum controls inclusive/exclusive edge of Recurrence iteration
patterns.description: IterateFromNextAfter and IterateFromPrevBefore accept a Boundary argument. Inclusive returns t if it falls on a boundary; Exclusive skips to next/prev. Always use constants timeutil.Inclusive or timeutil.Exclusive.

patterns.name: OpenPeriod uses *time.Time — nil means open/unbounded; touching periods do NOT overlap
patterns.description: Nil From means open start; nil To means open end. Touching periods that share only an endpoint return nil from Intersection — this is intentional: [t,t] has zero length.
patterns.example:
// OpenPeriod{From: &t1, To: nil} extends to infinity
// Intersection of [before,now] and [now,after] returns nil (they touch, not overlap)

patterns.name: Use ISODuration for month/year recurrences, never time.Duration arithmetic
patterns.description: Month and year lengths vary; time.Duration math gives wrong billing period ends for variable-length months. RecurrencePeriodMonth/Year constants wrap ISODuration correctly.
patterns.example:
// Wrong: anchor.Add(30 * 24 * time.Hour) -- wrong for February
// Correct: timeutil.NewRecurrence(timeutil.RecurrencePeriodMonth, anchor)

key_file_guides.role: Period interface requiring all three Contains variants. All concrete types assert var _ Period = MyType{} at declaration.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Any new period type must implement all three methods; missing one causes compile errors at assertion sites.

key_file_guides.role: Fully-bounded [from, to] interval with Overlaps, OverlapsInclusive, Intersection, Truncate, Equal.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Overlaps returns false for exactly-touching periods ([1,2],[2,3]); use OverlapsInclusive when sequential billing periods must be treated as adjacent.

key_file_guides.role: Half or fully-open interval with Intersection, Difference, Union, IsSupersetOf using *time.Time pointers.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Intersection of touching OpenPeriods (sharing one endpoint) returns nil — intentional. Union with either period being fully open ({nil,nil}) propagates openness.

key_file_guides.role: Recurrence schedule with Anchor+Interval, GetPeriodAt, IterateFromNextAfter, IterateFromPrevBefore, and RecurrenceIterator for bidirectional stepping.
key_file_guides.watch_for: MAX_SAFE_ITERATIONS = 1_000_000 cap prevents infinite loops; fractional ISO durations cause addIntervalNTimes to error. Backward iteration past 1733 is not supported (explicit TODO).

key_file_guides.role: Generic sorted Timeline[T] of Timed[T] values with GetClosedPeriods (n-1 segments from n timestamps) and GetOpenPeriods (n+1 segments including open ends).
key_file_guides.watch_for: GetClosedPeriods with a single timestamp returns one zero-length period (From==To). GetOpenPeriods always returns len(times)+1 segments. NewTimeline sorts input — insertion order does not matter.

key_file_guides.role: StartBoundedPeriod with mandatory From and optional *time.Time To; implements Period.
key_file_guides.watch_for: ContainsExclusive treats From as exclusive start — different from ClosedPeriod.ContainsExclusive which has inclusive start via Contains.

anti_patterns: Using time.Duration arithmetic instead of ISODuration for month/year recurrences — month lengths vary and Duration math produces wrong billing period ends
Constructing Recurrence{} struct literal without calling NewRecurrence — skips Validate(), allowing zero anchor or non-positive interval to pass through silently
Assuming Overlaps and OverlapsInclusive are interchangeable — sequential billing periods must use Overlaps (returns false for touching) to avoid double-counting
Constructing OpenPeriod{From: &t, To: &t} expecting a valid point interval — Intersection treats same-start-and-end as zero-length and returns nil

decisions.decision: Contains uses half-open [from, to) semantics as the default
decisions.rationale: Half-open intervals compose without overlap for sequential billing windows: period N ends at T, period N+1 starts at T, Contains(T) is true for N+1 only, preventing double-counting.

decisions.decision: Recurrence iterates from a floating anchor rather than an epoch
decisions.rationale: Subscription anchors can be any date; billing periods must align to the original subscription start regardless of when the calculation runs.

code_examples.scenario: Find the billing period containing a given usage timestamp using monthly recurrence
